methods.description: The technique used for layer counting in ice cores, involving visual stratigraphy, electrolytic conductivity, and insoluble dust particle concentration to assign annual cycles. This method is used to determine the age of ice layers by identifying annual markers.

steps.description: The parameters used for assignment of annual cycles included visual stratigraphy, electrolytic conductivity and insoluble dust particle concentration.
steps.searchable_summary: Assign annual cycles using visual stratigraphy, electrolytic conductivity, and dust concentration

steps.description: Annual layers in sodium were not observed due to diffusion in the ice as well as the limited sampling resolution.
steps.searchable_summary: Account for diffusion and resolution limitations in sodium layer observation

steps.description: Annual cycles in ammonium were identifiable only during the interstadials, when greater concentrations and thicker layers enabled their identification.
steps.searchable_summary: Identify ammonium annual cycles during interstadials

steps.description: Three people independently evaluated the data to produce three annual layer counting results.
steps.searchable_summary: Conduct independent evaluations of annual layer counting

steps.description: The three independent counting results were then evaluated again to produce a consistent final counting result.
steps.searchable_summary: Consolidate independent counting results into a final result

steps.description: The maximum counting error (MCE) was calculated as the sum of the 'uncertain' annual layers, and can be considered equivalent to a 2σ uncertainty interval.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate maximum counting error as sum of uncertain layers

steps.description: For the sections where ice was not available to measure, the layer thickness has been linearly interpolated from the adjacent measured sections.
steps.searchable_summary: Interpolate layer thickness for unmeasured sections
